provenance.title: DeepVol: volatility forecasting from high-frequency data with dilated causal convolutions
affiliations: Oxford-Man Institute of Quantitative Finance, University of Oxford, Oxford, UK
Signal Processing and Learning Group, Universidad Carlos III de Madrid, Madrid, Spain
Machine Learning Research Group, University of Oxford, Oxford, UK
provenance.abstract: Volatility forecasts play a central role among equity risk measures. We propose DeepVol, a model based on Dilated Causal Convolutions that uses high-frequency data to forecast day-ahead volatility. Our empirical findings demonstrate that dilated convolutional filters are highly effective at extracting relevant information from intraday financial time-series.
key_claims: DeepVol uses raw high-frequency intraday data directly, bypassing precomputed realised measures
Dilated Causal Convolutions (DCC) effectively handle long intraday sequences without proportional parameter growth
DeepVol outperforms GARCH-family, HEAVY, HAR, ARFIMA, MLP, and LSTM baselines on NASDAQ-100
Optimal sampling frequency is 5 minutes and optimal receptive field is 1 prior trading day
DeepVol generalises to out-of-distribution stocks, demonstrating transfer learning capability

architecture.description: Hierarchical stack of Dilated Causal Convolution (DCC) blocks followed by an attention mechanism and linear output head. Inspired by WaveNet.

variants.name: DeepVol
variants.description: Full model operating on raw high-frequency intraday returns

variants.name: DeepVol-RM
variants.description: Same DCC architecture but takes precomputed realised variance over 22 days as input instead of raw intraday data

modules.name: InputProjection
modules.description: Projects raw intraday return scalars into residual channel space
modules.note: Input layout (concatenated days) inferred from rolling-window description; exact 1D vs 2D layout assumed

modules.name: DilatedCausalConvBlock
modules.description: Single WaveNet-style residual block: dilated causal conv → gated activation → 1x1 conv → residual + skip
modules.dilation_schedule: d = 2^l for layer l within a block; blocks repeat the schedule

modules.name: SkipAggregation
modules.description: Sums all skip connection outputs across all layers and blocks

modules.name: AttentionMechanism
modules.description: Bahdanau-style additive attention over the time dimension to weight each time-step's importance
modules.operation: Additive attention (Bahdanau et al. 2014) — NOT self-attention/Transformer
modules.note: Paper explicitly says classic Bahdanau attention, not self-attention

modules.name: OutputHead
modules.description: Two linear layers with ReLU to project attended representation to scalar volatility forecast
modules.note: Equation 27 shows weighted sum of ReLU-activated convolution outputs; exact MLP structure inferred

connections: raw_returns → InputProjection → DilatedCausalConvBlock[0..L] → SkipAggregation → AttentionMechanism → OutputHead → sigma2_forecast

ambiguities.description: Exact architecture of output head beyond 'weights applied to convolutional operations' is not fully specified
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Two-layer MLP with ReLU following skip aggregation and attention

ambiguities.description: Whether input is flattened T*J sequence or structured as 2D [T, J] and then reshaped
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Flattened 1D sequence of length T*J fed to DCC stack
alternatives: 2D input with day dimension handled separately

variables.p_{i,t}: last price in ith interval on day t
variables.r_{i,t}: return over interval i on day t

equations.name: Realised Variance
equations.role: target proxy measure / baseline input feature

variables.r_{i,t}: ith intraday return for day t

variables.alpha_l: learnable weight for layer l output

equations.name: QLIKE Loss
equations.role: primary training loss and evaluation metric

tensors.name: raw_returns
tensors.description: Concatenated intraday returns over T past days, J intraday intervals per day (J=78 at 5-min freq for 6.5hr trading day)

tensors.name: attended_context
tensors.description: After Bahdanau attention collapses the time dimension

tensors.name: realised_variance_target
tensors.description: Realised variance computed via Eq. 2 at 5-min frequency

tensor_semantics.notes: J=78 for 5-min data (390 min / 5 = 78 intervals). T=1 (receptive field of 1 day) for optimal model.

optimizer.name: Adam
optimizer.note: Adam (Kingma & Ba 2014). Weight decay not specified — assumed 0.

datasets.name: NASDAQ-100 Out-of-Distribution Stocks
datasets.description: Train on half the tickers, test on unseen tickers (includes COVID volatility shock)

statistical_tests: Model Confidence Set (MCS) at 5% significance level

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Gated activation in DCC blocks uses tanh * sigmoid (WaveNet-style), not plain ReLU
implementation_assumptions.basis: Standard WaveNet architecture; paper cites Oord et al. 2016 as inspiration

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Input is a 1D sequence of length T*J (78 for 5-min, 1-day receptive field) after flattening intraday intervals
implementation_assumptions.basis: DCC operates on sequences; rolling-window approach described in Section 2.1.2
alternatives: 2D input with day/interval axes treated separately

implementation_assumptions.basis: Not mentioned in paper; standard default

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Kernel size = 3 across all DCC layers (explicitly stated in Table 1)
Dilation factor doubles per layer: d = 2^l for l in 0..5 within each block (6 layers, 2 blocks)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Standard WaveNet dilation schedule; paper describes exponential increase in receptive field

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Residual channels=32, dilation channels=64, skip channels=128, end channels=64 as per Table 1
Data normalisation: returns are used as-is (log returns are approximately zero-mean); no explicit normalisation layer mentioned
implementation_assumptions.basis: Section 2.1 describes log return calculation; no normalisation mentioned

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Multi-asset training: all NASDAQ-100 tickers are concatenated and treated as a single dataset (global model)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper describes training on all NASDAQ-100 tickers with shared parameters

ambiguities.description: Exact output head architecture beyond weighted ReLU sum not fully described
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Two-layer MLP with ReLU after skip aggregation and attention pooling

ambiguities.description: Whether attention is applied before or after skip connection summation
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Attention applied after skip sum, operating over the temporal dimension

ambiguities.description: Exact number of NASDAQ-100 tickers used (index composition varies); Tables A1/A2 list ~90 stocks
ambiguities.primary_assumption: 90 stocks as listed in Table A1
